docs: add AGENTS.md for AI coding assistants

Provides orientation for AI agents (build instructions, coding style,
repository layout, CI notes, and key concepts) so they can work
effectively on the codebase without having to rediscover conventions
from scratch.

ci: pre-commit hook for clang-format + atomic commit discipline (#6)

- Add .githooks/pre-commit: auto-runs clang-format -i on staged .cpp/.h
  files and re-stages them so every commit lands already formatted.
  Skips gracefully when clang-format is not on PATH (cloud agent runner).

- Update CMakeLists.txt: execute_process() calls git config core.hooksPath
  .githooks at configure time, so agents get the hook wired automatically
  the moment they run cmake --preset debug. No manual install step needed.

- Update AGENTS.md:
  - Document git hooks are automatic (no manual step)
  - Add explicit format-before-commit and tidy-before-push docker run
    commands for cloud agents where the hook skips
  - Add Commit discipline section under Conventions: atomic commits,
    commit-as-you-go, green-before-commit, Conventional Commits style

Co-authored-by: Tran Xuan Loc <tranxuanloc19920@gmail.com>
Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

ci: add copilot-setup-steps workflow and cloud agent docs (#4)

- Add .github/workflows/copilot-setup-steps.yml that builds the
  loxpp-dev:latest Docker image from the Dockerfile using GHA cache.
  Copilot cloud agent picks this up before starting, so it can use
  docker run for all build/test/lint operations.

- Trigger the workflow also on Dockerfile changes so the cached image
  stays in sync with toolchain updates.

- Add a 'Copilot cloud agent' section to AGENTS.md documenting the
  docker run pattern and reinforcing that Dockerfile is the single
  source of truth.

refactor: Allocator abstraction + ObjHandle in Value (#3)

* refactor: introduce Allocator abstraction + ObjHandle in Value

Phase 1 — Allocator abstraction:
- Add src/allocator.h: abstract Allocator interface + ObjHandle struct
  (index + cached ObjType; manual operator== for C++17 compat)
- Add src/simple_allocator.h/.cpp: SimpleAllocator owning vector<Obj*>
  and intern Table; makeString interns via hash, deref is O(1) by index;
  collect() is a stub; destructor deletes all Obj*
- Remove makeString() free function from object.h/.cpp
- Remove allocateObj<T>() template from memory.h (now private to allocator)
- Compiler/compile() replace vector<unique_ptr<Obj>>* + Table* with Allocator*
- VM replace m_objects + m_strings with unique_ptr<Allocator>; expose allocator()

Phase 2 — ObjHandle in Value:
- Value variant changes: Obj* -> ObjHandle
- isObj/isString work via cached ObjType in handle (no deref needed)
- asObj/asObjString gain Allocator& parameter for deref
- stringify/printValue: primary overload takes Allocator&; debug overload
  (no allocator) shows <str#N> for handles — used by chunk disassembly
  and VM debug trace
- Compiler::string() stores ObjHandle directly in constants (no deref bridge)
- VM Op::ADD: pop ObjHandle, deref for .chars, push new ObjHandle result
- test_table.cpp: SetObjPtrValue -> SetObjHandleValue using ObjHandle values;
  fixtures use SimpleAllocator instead of vector<unique_ptr<Obj>>
- test_allocator.cpp: 8 Phase 1 tests + 5 Phase 2 tests (Value holds ObjHandle)
- test_harness.cpp: compile_to_bytecode uses SimpleAllocator; eval_expr_str
  uses allocator-based stringify; compile() now takes Allocator*

All 6 test suites pass (debug + release).

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: apply clang-format to all modified files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* review: address PR comments

- compiler.cpp: rename ObjHandle h -> handle (avoid single-char name)
- vm.cpp: add TODO comment on string concat alloc vs book's memcpy approach
- vm.h: restore 'For testing/debugging only' comment on m_lastResult
- test_harness.cpp: add TODO to replace ad-hoc disassembler switch

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

commit.message:
feat: implement hash table (Chapter 20) (#2)

* feat: implement hash table with open addressing and string interning

- Implement Table with open addressing + linear probing (TABLE_MAX_LOAD=0.75)
- FNV-1a hash function (hashString) exposed from table.h
- Tombstone-based deletion to preserve probe chain integrity
- tableSet/tableGet/tableDelete/tableAddAll/tableFindString all implemented
- ObjString hash field set in makeString via FNV-1a
- makeString accepts optional Table* for transparent string interning
- Thread intern table through compile() and Compiler for string literals
- VM holds Table m_strings; all strings (literals + concat) are interned
- Obj* equality in value.cpp simplified to pointer comparison (valid post-intern)
- table.cpp added to loxpp, test_parser_vm, test_parser_bytecode targets

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* refactor: adopt OOP Table interface (private vector inheritance)

- Replace C-style struct Table + free functions with a class using
  private std::vector<Entry> inheritance, mirroring Chunk's design
- Public API: set/get/del/addAll/findString (method style)
- RAII replaces initTable/freeTable — no manual lifecycle management
- findBucket is a private static helper; adjustCapacity rehashes internally
- Fix: Entry::value defaults to Nil{} so empty buckets are correctly
  distinguished from tombstones (bool{true}) in findBucket
- Update test_table.cpp to use new method API while preserving all
  test cases and their spirit (48 tests, same 9 categories)
- Update object.cpp callers: tableFindString/tableSet → strings->findString/set

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: apply clang-format to all C++ sources

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------

Co-authored-by: Tran Xuan Loc <tranxuanloc19920@gmail.com>

commit.message:
test: comprehensive hash table test suite (TDD skeleton) (#1)

* test: add hash table skeleton and comprehensive test suite

- Add uint32_t hash field to ObjString (required by all table ops)
- Add src/table.h: Entry/Table structs and full public API declarations
- Add src/table.cpp: stub implementations (compile-only, tests will fail)
- Add test/test_table.cpp: 48 tests across 9 categories covering:
  * ObjString hash field stability and FNV-1a properties
  * initTable / freeTable lifecycle
  * tableSet: new key, overwrite, all Value types
  * tableGet: empty table, missing key, existing key, output param
  * tableDelete: observable removal, probe-chain integrity
  * Collision/probing: 2-way, 3-way, mixed, stress (200 entries)
  * tableAddAll: empty/non-empty source, overlap, no mutation of src
  * tableFindString: raw char lookup, hash-collision correctness
  * String interning: same content -> same pointer, many strings
- Register test_table binary in test/CMakeLists.txt

Tests intentionally fail until the real implementation is filled in.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: address review comments on test_table.cpp

- Replace real FNV-1a with a fake hash (first byte of string) to make
  collision engineering trivial and deterministic
- Simplify collidingPair/collidingN: any two strings starting with
  the same character are guaranteed collisions — no brute-force needed
- Use makeString() factory instead of manually constructing ObjStrings
  throughout all helpers and inline allocations
- Remove DifferentContentDifferentHash: tests hash diffusion, not the table
- Remove HashIsNonZeroForNonEmptyString: tests hash function, not the table
- Remove HashIsStableAcrossTwoCalls: tests hash function, not the table

28 tests still fail as expected with stub implementations.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* test: remove HashFieldTest section per review

The section was testing the fake hash function's own behaviour, not the
table. Removed entirely.

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

* style: apply clang-format to table files

Co-authored-by: Copilot <223556219+Copilot@users.noreply.github.com>

---------
